cards.front: What does the coefficient represent in the context of logistic regression?
cards.back: The odds ratio of a predictor variable to the response variable

cards.front: What is the difference between a coefficient and an odds ratio?
cards.back: A coefficient represents the change in the log-odds of the response variable for a one-unit change in the predictor variable, while an odds ratio represents the change in the odds of the response variable for a one-unit change in the predictor variable

cards.front: What is the formula to calculate the odds ratio from a coefficient?
What is the purpose of standardizing coefficients in logistic regression?
cards.back: To ensure that the coefficients are comparable across different predictor variables

cards.front: Why are odd ratios often used in place of coefficients in logistic regression?
cards.back: Because they provide a clearer interpretation of the effect size and are less affected by the scale of the predictor variables
